content.problemStatement:
Chef wants to impress Chefina but she gave him a problem. Chef is not good in problem-solving so he wants your help to find the minimum of (A⊕X)+(A⊕Y)(A⊕X) + (A⊕Y)(A⊕X)+(A⊕Y) for any AAA, where ⊕ is Bit-wise XOR.

Input:
First-line will contain TTT, the number of test cases. Then the test cases follow:
Each test case contains a single line of input, two integers X,YX, YX,Y.
Output:For each test case, output in a single line minimum of (A⊕X)+(A⊕Y)(A⊕X) + (A⊕Y)(A⊕X)+(A⊕Y).
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Each of the next T lines contains two space‑separated integers X and Y.
content.outputFormat: For each test case output a single line containing the minimum possible value of (A⊕X)+(A⊕Y).

sampleTestCases.explanation: X xor Y = 6 ^ 12 = 10, which is the minimum achievable value.
For (5,5) xor is 0. For (1,2) xor is 3.

solution.approach: For each bit position consider the bits xi and yi of X and Y. If xi == yi we can choose ai equal to that bit and the contribution of this position to the sum becomes 0. If xi != yi, whichever value we pick for ai the contribution is exactly 1·2^i. Therefore the minimal total sum equals the sum of 2^i over all positions where xi and yi differ, i.e. X xor Y. So the answer for each test case is simply X ^ Y.
solution.timeComplexity: O(1) per test case, O(T) overall.
